latest.changelog:
### Fixes
- Fix "TV Status Unknown" flickering on the dashboard — CEC queries
  frequently time out due to slow cec-client invocations, which was
  clobbering the cached status on every failed poll. Now keeps the
  last known good status and only shows "unknown" after 3 consecutive
  failures

### Improvements
- CEC status updates faster after sending power commands — fast
  polling window extended from 10s to 30s to cover slow TVs, and
  active source query is skipped during fast polling to halve
  per-poll latency

releases.notes:
### Fixes
- Fix "TV Status Unknown" flickering on the dashboard — CEC queries
  frequently time out due to slow cec-client invocations, which was
  clobbering the cached status on every failed poll. Now keeps the
  last known good status and only shows "unknown" after 3 consecutive
  failures

### Improvements
- CEC status updates faster after sending power commands — fast
  polling window extended from 10s to 30s to cover slow TVs, and
  active source query is skipped during fast polling to halve
  per-poll latency

### Improvements
- CEC status updates faster after sending power commands — fast
  polling window extended from 10s to 30s to cover slow TVs, and
  active source query is skipped during fast polling to halve
  per-poll latency

### New Feature: Live CEC TV Status Monitor
- CEC TV power status now updates automatically via a background
  monitor — no more stale "TV is Off" after sending power commands
- After any CEC command, status polls every 1 second for 10 seconds
  to catch the TV's response, then resumes normal 30-second polling
- Dashboard TV Control panel now shows:
  - Transition states ("TV Turning On..." / "TV Turning Off...") with
    a pulsing amber indicator
  - TV name and HDMI port (e.g. "SAMSUNG · HDMI 2")
  - Active source indicator (whether the Pi is the current input)
- CEC status changes are broadcast via WebSocket — the dashboard
  updates live without manual refresh

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Fix animated posters flashing to static poster during transitions —
  keeps the animated poster playing through the TRANSITION phase until
  the next movie loads

### Improvements
- Animated posters now fade out smoothly over the last 1 second of
  the display cycle before the next movie transitions in
- Poster display time minimum lowered from 5 to 3 seconds

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Fix animated posters flashing to static poster during transitions —
  keeps the animated poster playing through the TRANSITION phase until
  the next movie loads

### Improvements
- Animated posters now fade out smoothly over the last 1 second of
  the display cycle before the next movie transitions in

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Fix animated posters flashing to static poster during transitions —
  keeps the animated poster playing through the TRANSITION phase until
  the next movie loads

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Revert animated poster display changes from v1.0.33-v1.0.35 —
  restores original animated poster rendering behavior

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing

### Improvements
- Animated posters now use the same rendering pipeline as static
  posters. Transitions (crossfade, wipe, dissolve) apply correctly
  when entering and leaving an animated poster, video loops seamlessly
  without fade-to-black gaps, and poster display timing is consistent

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Fix animated posters flashing to static poster during transitions —
  the display was stopping the animated poster video at the start of
  the TRANSITION phase, showing the static poster for 2 seconds before
  the next movie. Now keeps the animated poster playing through the
  transition until the next movie loads

### Fixes
- Fix Spotlight (manual) mode getting permanently stuck on a movie —
  the manual cycle task was cancelling itself when trying to resume
  idle mode, preventing the MANUAL→IDLE transition from completing
- Fix animated posters only playing once before reverting to static —
  movies without trailers were using the pre-trailer duration (6s)
  instead of the playlist display duration (default 30s), leaving
  too little time for the animated poster loop-fade cycle

### Fixes
- Fix banner text overflowing when Plex or Jellyfin playback starts —
  the banner font size is now recalculated to fit the integration's
  banner text and font within the display width
- Fix webhook URL copy button failing on HTTP connections — added
  clipboard fallback for insecure origins where navigator.clipboard
  is unavailable
- Fix ACTIVE banner hardcoded to "Now Playing" instead of using the
  per-integration banner text from settings

### Improvements
- Integration banner font is now sent to the display when playback
  starts, and restored to the idle font when playback stops

### Fixes
- Fix kiosk display not restarting after OTA update — the restart was
  racing the symlink swap, so the kiosk reloaded old code. Now restarts
  after the slot swap completes.

### Fixes
- Fix info row icons blinking when display settings change — settings
  refreshes no longer trigger the fade-in animation meant for movie
  transitions

### Improvements
- Bump default banner font size from 3vh to 6vh for better visibility
  on fresh installs
- Restart kiosk display automatically during OTA update swap so updated
  display code takes effect without manual intervention

### Housekeeping
- Remove obsolete docs (MAINTENANCE.md, PI5_DISPLAY.md) superseded by
  internal reference files

### Improvements
- Format badge icons (Dolby Atmos, DTS:X, etc.) are now vertically
  centered on the info row instead of bottom-aligned — rectangular
  badges no longer sit lower than square ones

### New Features
- In-app "Upgrade to Premiere" flow for Matinee users — select a plan,
  enter your email, and check out directly from the License tab
- New `POST /api/license/checkout-url` endpoint builds a prefilled
  Lemon Squeezy checkout URL with the Pi's serial embedded

### Improvements
- Removed hardcoded default PIN from provision.sh (fresh installs now
  require first-run setup)
- CLAUDE.md untracked from the repository

### Tests
- Fixed 28 pre-existing E2E test selector drifts
- Hardened reload flakiness in settings E2E tests

### New Features
- In-app "Upgrade to Premiere" flow for Matinee users — select a plan,
  enter your email, and check out directly from the License tab
- New `POST /api/license/checkout-url` endpoint builds a prefilled
  Lemon Squeezy checkout URL with the Pi's serial embedded

### Improvements
- Removed hardcoded default PIN from provision.sh (fresh installs now
  require first-run setup)
- CLAUDE.md untracked from the repository

### Tests
- Fixed 28 pre-existing E2E test selector drifts
- Hardened reload flakiness in settings E2E tests

### New Features
- In-app "Upgrade to Premiere" flow for Matinee users — select a plan,
  enter your email, and check out directly from the License tab
- New `POST /api/license/checkout-url` endpoint builds a prefilled
  Lemon Squeezy checkout URL with the Pi's serial embedded

### Improvements
- Removed hardcoded default PIN from provision.sh (fresh installs now
  require first-run setup)
- CLAUDE.md untracked from the repository

### Tests
- Fixed 28 pre-existing E2E test selector drifts
- Hardened reload flakiness in settings E2E tests

### Security
- Expanded PyArmor obfuscation from 51 to 57 modules (Tier 2d)
- Added `content_filter`, `network_monitor`, `system_refresh`,
  `websocket_manager`, `color_palette`, and `image_processing`
- All remaining plain-source Python files (models, schemas, config)
  are now minified with `python-minifier`: comments and docstrings
  stripped, locals renamed, imports combined. Annotations are preserved
  so Pydantic and SQLAlchemy keep working.

### Security
- Expanded PyArmor obfuscation from 46 to 51 modules (Tier 2c)
- Now also protects `backend/main.py` (FastAPI entry point, WebSocket
  handler, middleware) and the display renderer (`pygame_display.py`,
  `gl_poc.py`, `gl_utils.py`, `gl_transitions.py`)
- Both backend service and kiosk display process are now running
  bytecode-encrypted Python

### Security
- Expanded PyArmor obfuscation from 44 to 46 modules (Tier 2b)
- Now also protects `routes/auth.py` and `routes/license.py`
- CI now stages files preserving directory structure, so basename
  collisions between `core/` and `routes/` modules no longer limit
  which files can be obfuscated

### Security
- Expanded PyArmor obfuscation from 32 to 44 modules (Tier 2a)
- Now protects API clients (TMDB, OMDb, Wikidata, MPV) and all remaining
  route handlers (system, backup, settings, movies, storage, logs, support)
- Core auth helpers (bcrypt, JWT, rate limiting) also obfuscated

### Security
- Expanded PyArmor obfuscation from 13 to 32 modules
- Now protects: state machine, update manager, auto-cacher, playlist scheduler,
  storage janitor, metadata enrichment, RPi LED protocol, and all CRUD route handlers
- Proprietary tier quota and slideshow logic no longer readable in plain text

### Security
- Stripped proprietary docs, dev tools, and internal files from public release tarball
- Removed all prior releases from public GitHub repo

### Improvements
- Release tarball now contains only user-facing files: app code, deploy scripts, user docs, fonts

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed login prompt appearing on Hardware tab for Matinee users (tier 403 vs auth 403)
- Fixed releases.json not including current version in shipped tarball
- Fixed provision.sh WiFi grep crash with pipefail on ethernet-only Pi
- Fixed service ReadWritePaths crash on fresh install (no A/B slots)

### Improvements
- Manifest URL field is now read-only by default with Edit/Lock toggle
- Backend validates manifest URL before saving (must return valid JSON)
- Provisioning clears apt and pip caches (~460 MB saved)

### Tests
- 27 auth tests: setup wizard, login, logout, rate limiting, JWT, HTTPS
- 74 auth matrix tests: all auth modes x all endpoints x tier gate distinction

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed fresh install crash: WiFi grep pipeline with pipefail on ethernet-only Pi
- Fixed service startup failure on fresh install: A/B slot directories now optional in ReadWritePaths
- Fixed deprovision user removal when display process is still running
- Web installer now correctly runs provision.sh on fresh Pi (was only calling install.sh)

### New
- Added deprovision.sh for complete htMarquee removal (supports --dry-run and --full)

### New Features
- Public README with screenshots pushed to GitHub on each release
- Web install script for curl | bash installs from htmarquee.com

### Bug Fixes
- Font size and color now survive reboots, service restarts, and system refresh
- Fixed font settings not passed to SlideshowState constructor
- Fixed font settings missing from generate_upcoming_playlist startup path
- Sudoers entry for OTA updates added to install script
- Service capabilities (CAP_SETUID, CAP_SETGID, CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE) for sudo during OTA
- Migrated old Forgejo manifest URL to GitHub for existing installations

### Tests
- 93 new tests: OTA update manager, deploy validation, fonts API, settings pipeline, release notes
- Deploy validation catches missing sudo capabilities and sudoers entries

### Infrastructure
- Repo cleanup: non-app files moved to _local/ (gitignored)
- All Forgejo URLs migrated to HTTPS
- CI runner updated for HTTPS with SSL verify skip in containers

### New Features
- Adjustable banner font size with slider (1.0–8.0vh range)
- Banner font color picker — choose any color for the header text
- Public update distribution — all Pis now pull updates from GitHub
- Default manifest URL set automatically for new and existing installs

### Improvements
- TMDB and OMDb API key requirements added to product website
- Forgejo instance migrated to HTTPS across all CI and documentation
- Deploy workflow updated with display/ and fonts/ sync reminders

### Infrastructure
- CI pipeline now publishes obfuscated releases to GitHub (htMarquee/htMarquee_app)
- Manifest and release notes pushed to GitHub for public access
- Website stripped to static-only for initial launch (chat widget deferred)

### New Features
- Customizable banner fonts for the display header
- 8 built-in Google Fonts: Audiowide, Barlow Condensed, Bitcount Grid Double, Doto, Geo, Monoton, Sixtyfour, Zen Tokyo Zoo
- Upload custom .ttf/.otf/.woff/.woff2 font files for the banner
- Separate font selection for idle, Plex, and Jellyfin playback banners
- Font changes hot-apply via WebSocket — no restart needed
- Release notes now displayed in the Software Update section
- Current version notes shown with expandable release history

### Bug Fixes
- Rollback progress bar now advances instead of staying stuck at 0%
- Rollback button grayed out when no previous version is available

### LED & Website Fixes
- LED off now clears entire strip regardless of configured count
- Fixed OTA slot cleanup permission errors
- Fixed 11 website bugs: favicon, mobile CTA, CORS, OG tags, accessibility
- Fixed 20+ inaccuracies in product website and chat API knowledge base

### LED UI & OTA Improvements
- Fixed LED sections not graying out when GPIO disabled
- OTA updates now install Pi-specific dependencies automatically

### LED Strip Control
- LED Strip Control grays out when GPIO disabled
- GPIO toggle shows restart prompt with one-click restart button
- Added GPIO pinout diagram to hardware settings

### Bug Fix
- Fixed license tier detection — UI no longer shows matinee locks with a valid Premiere license

### Security
- Improved license code obfuscation with per-build random identifier renaming

### Version Stamping
- App now correctly reports its release version instead of always showing v1.0.0

### Build Pipeline
- Initial release pipeline with PyArmor obfuscation, Ed25519 signing, and OTA manifest generation
